description: You are a bioinformatics scientist studying extremophiles. Your goal is to identify which of the uncharacterized proteins in ~/UGENE_Data/hmmer_task/uncharacterized_proteome.fasta is a Cytochrome c homolog using the training sequences provided in ~/UGENE_Data/hmmer_task/cytochrome_c_multispecies.fasta. In UGENE, generate a multiple sequence alignment of the training data and save it to ~/UGENE_Data/hmmer_task/results/cytc_aligned.fasta. Build an HMM profile from this alignment, saved as ~/UGENE_Data/hmmer_task/results/cytc_domain.hmm, and use it to scan the uncharacterized proteome. Export the identified sequence annotations to ~/UGENE_Data/hmmer_task/results/hmm_hits.gff. Document your findings in a report at ~/UGENE_Data/hmmer_task/results/discovery_report.txt including the HMM profile length (consensus positions), the exact FASTA header of the identified homolog, and the start and end amino acid coordinates of the match.
